dataset: NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis Monthly Long-Term Mean Surface Air Temperature

pi_items.source: Matches role and department specified in setup_task.sh

Matches DATA_FILE variable in setup_task.sh and task.json

Matches actual NCEP Reanalysis air.mon.ltm.nc value of ~19.48°C at 25N 80E for January, which falls in this range. Used in verifier.py.
Matches actual NCEP Reanalysis air.mon.ltm.nc value of ~-21.52°C at 35N 80E for January, which falls in this range. Used in verifier.py.
Matches actual difference of ~41.0°C in the dataset, which is well above the 20.0°C threshold set in verifier.py.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires analyzing the NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis Monthly Long-Term Mean Surface Air Temperature dataset, which is a real-world scientific dataset, not synthetic. Python script analysis of the 'air.mon.ltm.nc' file verifies the January (month 0) 'air' variable temperature at Northern India (25N, 80E) is ~19.48°C, while the Tibetan Plateau (35N, 80E) is ~-21.52°C. These actual values align perfectly with the target plausibility bounds given in the metadata (India: 10-25°C, Tibet: -35 to -5°C). The true temperature gradient is roughly 41°C, successfully exceeding the minimum difference threshold of 20°C specified by the verifier.
